channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Ex-Secret Service agent and conservative media personality Dan Bongino picked as FBI deputy director
item.description: Dan Bongino, a former U.S. Secret Service agent who has penned best-selling books, ran unsuccessfully for office and gained fame as a conservative pundit with TV shows and a popular podcast, has been chosen to serve as FBI deputy director.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Trump administration is putting USAID staffers on leave worldwide and firing at least 1,600
item.description: The Trump administration says it's placing all but a fraction of staffers at the U.S. Agency for International Development on leave worldwide and eliminating at least 1,600 U.S.-based jobs.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump set to meet with world leaders in DC during busy week as Russia-Ukraine peace talks continue
item.description: President Trump will meet with the French president and U.K. prime minister this week as administration leaders continue efforts to reach a peace deal between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump and Musk aren't the first to make deep cuts. Clinton-era Reinventing Government saved billions
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A new administration swept into Washington and announced plans to shake it up, using corporate know-how and new technology to streamline the federal bureaucracy. It offered millions of government employees buyouts and slashed costs to balance the budget.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth defends Trump's firings of Pentagon leaders and says there may be more dismissals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth insists President Donald Trump ’s abrupt firing of the nation’s senior military officer amid a wave of dismissals at the Pentagon wasn't unusual, brushing aside outcry that the new administration is openly seeking to inject politics into the military.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk doubles down on email ultimatum after agencies push back
item.description: Newly-appointed FBI Director Kash Patel sent a message to the FBI workforce on Saturday night telling them to not reply to the emails.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: SecDef Hegseth responds to rumors he drafted 'list' of military officials he will purge
item.description: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth denied the existence of a "list" of planned senior military officials that he and the Trump administration planned to fire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump revels in mass federal firings and jeers at Biden before adoring conservative crowd
item.description: OXON HILL, Md. (AP) — President Donald Trump said Saturday that “nobody's ever seen anything" like his administration's sweeping effort to fire thousands of federal employees and shrink the size of government, congratulating himself for “dominating” Washington and sending bureaucrats “packing.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump-Putin summit preparations are underway, Russia says
item.description: Preparations are underway for a face-to-face meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin, Russia's deputy foreign minister said Saturday. It marked a clear departure from Western efforts to isolate Moscow over its war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A look at Dan 'Razin' Caine, Trump's pick to be the top US military officer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s choice to be the top U.S. military officer, retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan Caine, endeared himself to the commander in chief through his military call sign, “Razin,” during a meeting in Iraq years ago.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump DEI executive orders
item.description: U.S. District Judge Adam B. Abelson, a former President Biden appointee, issued a preliminary injunction on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: USDA to follow ‘president’s lead’ on foreign farm ownership policy
item.description: The concern, however, is that international ownership could lead to risks of spying or economic pressure.
item.dc:creator: Alex Caprariello, NewsNation

item.title: Frey vetoes council move toward a pedestrian plaza at George Floyd Square
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey has vetoed the City Council’s plan to explore building a pedestrian-only mall at George Floyd Square, the intersection where an officer killed Floyd in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: A military vet and a scientist were securing America’s food system. Trump fired them
item.description: More than 10,000 federal employees who had yet to complete their probationary periods have been fired by the Trump administration, including those who work to protect American agriculture.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration tries to bring back fired nuclear weapons workers, reversing DOGE cuts
item.description: he Trump administration has halted the firings of hundreds of federal employees who were tasked with working on the nation's nuclear weapons programs, in an about-face that has left workers confused and experts cautioning that DOGE's blind cost cutting will put communities at risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Washington Post backs out of 'Fire Elon Musk' ad order
item.description: The Washington Post this week backed out of a “Fire Elon Musk” advertising order that was to run as a wrap on some of its Tuesday editions, according to the advocacy group Common Cause.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5
